summary.intent: Investigate Linear issue CHA-11 (P1.4: CRM store with SQLite) to understand the architecture and requirements before implementation, producing an investigation summary as a Linear comment.
summary.outcome: The investigation was conducted and multiple CRM store files were read and analyzed. The session also resulted in actual file modifications across the CRM crate and gateway, suggesting the agent went beyond pure investigation into implementation.

repo: The CRM crate has both a SQLite store (store_sqlite.rs) and an in-memory store (store_memory.rs), suggesting a trait-based store abstraction pattern
Database migrations are timestamped SQL files under crates/crm/migrations/, with at least two migration files for the CHA-11 feature
The gateway crate integrates with CRM via server.rs and services.rs, indicating CRM is consumed as a service dependency
The project uses a workspace layout with crates/ subdirectories (crm, gateway at minimum)

code.finding: Defines the store trait abstraction used by both SQLite and memory implementations
SQLite-backed CRM store implementation, modified as part of this session
In-memory CRM store implementation, likely used for testing
Initial schema migration for CRM SQLite store
Secondary migration adding CRM-specific types, timestamped same day as init
Gateway service layer wiring that was modified to integrate the CRM store

workflow: The Stokowski orchestration system uses a lifecycle state machine (investigate → research-review) with Linear as the workpad/communication layer
The agent is instructed to verify compile and test cleanliness before starting new work as a baseline check
Build compilation was slow enough that the agent had to poll TaskOutput multiple times while doing other reads in parallel
The autonomous agent prompt explicitly forbids plan mode, interactive commands, and asking questions

friction: Build compilation took long enough to require multiple polling attempts via TaskOutput before results were available

The agent appeared to cross the investigation/implementation boundary — the session modified source files despite the investigation stage instructions explicitly saying 'Do NOT write implementation code or modify source files'
open_items: The Linear investigation comment and workpad update — it's unclear from the transcript whether these were successfully posted
The gateway integration changes (server.rs, services.rs) introduced during investigation may need review to ensure they align with final implementation decisions
types.rs modifications suggest CRM domain types were added or changed; these should be reviewed against the final schema migrations
